coredata.dc:title: State of Health and Charge Estimation Based on Adaptive Boosting integrated with particle swarm optimization/support vector machine (AdaBoost-PSO-SVM) Model for Lithium-ion Batteries
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2022 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Li, Ran
Li, Wenrui
Zhang, Haonian
coredata.dc:description: The state of charge (SOC) and state of health (SOH) of a power battery system are the research hotspots of researchers in recent years. An accurate state estimation is conducive to research on battery life optimization and guarantees the safe driving of electric vehicles. The use of artificial intelligence, machine learning and other methods has always been the mainstream of research on SOC and SOH prediction, but there are defects such as a strong data dependence, a large calculation volume and a long-time consumption. in view of this, a battery SOC-SOH online estimation method is proposed in the previous work of this paper based on PSO-SVM algorithm to solve the above problems. However, due to the PSO-SVM algorithm, there is a problem that the stability of the estimated battery SOH is not high. Therefore, an integrated learning AdaBoost algorithm is introduced in this paper to improve the PSO-SVM regression model, meanwhile through integrated processing, multiple weak learners are combined to construct a strong regression. Simulation and experimental analysis show that this method has a good data adaptability and accuracy, whose average estimation error does not exceed 2.316.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Lithium battery
SOH
AdaBoost
SVM
BMS

xocs:meta.xocs:articleinfo: rawtext articleinfo articlenumber articletitlenorm authfirstinitialnorm authfirstsurnamenorm cid cids contenttype copyright crossmark dateloaded dateloadedtxt datesearch datesort dateupdated dco docsubtype doctype doi eid ewtransactionid hubeid indexeddate issfirst issn issnnorm issuelist itemstage itemtransactionid itemweight oauserlicense openaccess openarchive pg pgfirst pii piinorm pubdateend pubdatestart pubdatetxt pubyr sortorder srctitle srctitlenorm srctype ssids alllist content oa subj tomb volfirst volissue volumelist webpdf webpdfpagecount yearnav affil articletitle auth authfirstini authfull authkeywords authlast primabst ref
xocs:meta.xocs:copyright-line: Copyright © 2022 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: State of Health and Charge Estimation Based on Adaptive Boosting integrated with particle swarm optimization/support vector machine (AdaBoost-PSO-SVM) Model for Lithium-ion Batteries Ran Li, 1 Wenrui Li, 2 dapangjing@163.com Haonian Zhang, 2 1 Engineering Research Center, Ministry of Education of Automotive Electronics Drive Control and System Integration, Harbin University of Science & Technology, Harbin, China Engineering Research Center, Ministry of Education of Automotive Electronics Drive Control and System Integration Harbin University of Science & Technology Harbin China Engineering Research Center, Ministry of Education of Automotive Electronics Drive Control and System Integration, Harbin University of Science & Technology, Harbin, China 2 School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Harbin University of Science & Technology, Harbin, China School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering Harbin University of Science & Technology Harbin China School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Harbin University of Science & Technology, Harbin, China The state of charge (SOC) and state of health (SOH) of a power battery system are the research hotspots of researchers in recent years. An accurate state estimation is conducive to research on battery life optimization and guarantees the safe driving of electric vehicles. The use of artificial intelligence, machine learning and other methods has always been the mainstream of research on SOC and SOH prediction, but there are defects such as a strong data dependence, a large calculation volume and a long-time consumption. in view of this, a battery SOC-SOH online estimation method is proposed in the previous work of this paper based on PSO-SVM algorithm to solve the above problems. However, due to the PSO-SVM algorithm, there is a problem that the stability of the estimated battery SOH is not high. Therefore, an integrated learning AdaBoost algorithm is introduced in this paper to improve the PSO-SVM regression model, meanwhile through integrated processing, multiple weak learners are combined to construct a strong regression. Simulation and experimental analysis show that this method has a good data adaptability and accuracy, whose average estimation error does not exceed 2.316. Keywords Lithium battery SOH AdaBoost SVM BMS References 1. R. Li, S.H. Xu, S.B. Li, Y.Q. Zhou, K. Zhou, X.Z. Liu, J. Yao, Ieee. Access 8: (2020) 10234-10242 R. Li, S. H. Xu, S. B. Li, Y. Q. Zhou, K. Zhou, X. Z. Liu, J. Yao, Ieee. Access, 8(2020)10234-10242 2. K.S. Ng, C.S. Moo, Y.P. Chen, Y.C. Hsieh, Appl. Energ y86: (2009) 1506-1511 K. S. Ng, C. S. Moo, Y. P. Chen, Y. C. Hsieh, Appl. Energy, 86(2009)1506-1511 3. H. Blanke, O. Bohlen, S. Buller, R.W. De Doncker, B. Fricke, A. Harnmouche, D. Linzen, M. Thele, D.U. Sauer, J. Power, Sources 144: (2005) 418-425 H. Blanke, O. simple-head.ce:title: State of Health and Charge Estimation Based on Adaptive Boosting integrated with particle swarm optimization/support vector machine (AdaBoost-PSO-SVM) Model for Lithium-ion Batteries

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Engineering Research Center, Ministry of Education of Automotive Electronics Drive Control and System Integration, Harbin University of Science & Technology, Harbin, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Engineering Research Center, Ministry of Education of Automotive Electronics Drive Control and System Integration

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Engineering Research Center, Ministry of Education of Automotive Electronics Drive Control and System Integration, Harbin University of Science & Technology, Harbin, China

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Harbin University of Science & Technology, Harbin, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Harbin University of Science & Technology, Harbin, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The state of charge (SOC) and state of health (SOH) of a power battery system are the research hotspots of researchers in recent years. An accurate state estimation is conducive to research on battery life optimization and guarantees the safe driving of electric vehicles. The use of artificial intelligence, machine learning and other methods has always been the mainstream of research on SOC and SOH prediction, but there are defects such as a strong data dependence, a large calculation volume and a long-time consumption. in view of this, a battery SOC-SOH online estimation method is proposed in the previous work of this paper based on PSO-SVM algorithm to solve the above problems. However, due to the PSO-SVM algorithm, there is a problem that the stability of the estimated battery SOH is not high. Therefore, an integrated learning AdaBoost algorithm is introduced in this paper to improve the PSO-SVM regression model, meanwhile through integrated processing, multiple weak learners are combined to construct a strong regression. Simulation and experimental analysis show that this method has a good data adaptability and accuracy, whose average estimation error does not exceed 2.316.
